note: 65-year-old male presents for follow-up of type 2 diabetes mellitus and hypertension. Glycemic control suboptimal with HbA1c of 8.2. Blood pressure today 152/94. Continues metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 20mg daily. Will increase lisinopril to 40mg and add empagliflozin 10mg.
notes: E11.65 (T2DM with hyperglycemia) preferred over E11.9 because A1c indicates active poor control.

note: Patient is a 42-year-old woman with chief complaint of right-sided lower back pain radiating to the right leg, present for 3 weeks. Denies trauma. No history of malignancy. Exam reveals positive straight leg raise on the right at 45 degrees. Diagnosis: right-sided lumbar radiculopathy.
notes: M54.41 specifically codes right-sided lumbar radiculopathy with sciatica. Note 'no history of malignancy' is negated and should not trigger any malignancy codes.

note: 28-year-old female presents with acute pharyngitis, fever to 101.4F, and tender anterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Rapid strep test positive. Started on amoxicillin 500mg TID for 10 days.
notes: J02.0 is streptococcal pharyngitis.

note: Established patient seen for annual physical. No complaints. Vitals stable. BMI 31.4. Counseled on diet and exercise. Lipid panel and HbA1c ordered.
notes: Z00.00 for routine adult medical exam without abnormal findings; E66.9 for obesity (BMI >= 30) discovered on this visit.

note: Patient admitted from ED with acute exacerbation of COPD. Productive cough, increased dyspnea over 4 days. Oxygen saturation 88% on room air, improved to 94% on 2L NC. Chest X-ray without focal consolidation. Started on prednisone, azithromycin, and bronchodilator nebulizers.
notes: J44.1 = COPD with acute exacerbation. The note rules out pneumonia (no focal consolidation), so don't code J18.9.

note: 55-year-old man post-MI 3 months ago, here for cardiac follow-up. Stable on dual antiplatelet therapy, atorvastatin, metoprolol, and lisinopril. No chest pain, no shortness of breath, no edema. Echo from last week shows EF 45%.
notes: I25.2 = old MI; I50.32 = chronic diastolic (or here, reduced EF) heart failure. The 'no chest pain' is negated and shouldn't trigger angina codes.

note: Patient with known major depressive disorder, recurrent, presents with worsening depressive symptoms over 6 weeks. PHQ-9 score 18. Denies suicidal ideation. Currently on sertraline 100mg. Increasing to 150mg and adding referral to psychotherapy.
notes: F33.2 = MDD recurrent severe without psychotic features (PHQ-9 18 = severe). Note SI is negated.

note: 12-year-old with 2-day history of right ear pain, fever, decreased appetite. Otoscopic exam: right tympanic membrane erythematous and bulging. Diagnosis: acute right otitis media. Prescribed amoxicillin.
notes: H66.91 = otitis media, unspecified, right ear. (More specific suppurative codes if pus visible, but description here is generic.)

note: 72-year-old male former smoker (45 pack-years, quit 5 years ago) presenting for quarterly COPD follow-up. Reports mild exertional dyspnea, unchanged from baseline. No acute cough, no fever, no increase in sputum production. Spirometry from last month: FEV1/FVC 0.58, FEV1 62% predicted (GOLD 2). On tiotropium 18mcg daily and albuterol PRN. O2 sat 94% on room air. Impression: stable COPD, GOLD Group B.
notes: J44.9 = COPD, unspecified — appropriate for stable disease without acute exacerbation or concurrent lower respiratory infection. No fever or increased secretions to warrant J44.0 or J44.1.

note: 68-year-old woman with chronic systolic heart failure presenting for routine cardiology follow-up. Stable at baseline; mild exertional dyspnea unchanged, no dyspnea at rest, no new lower extremity edema, weight stable. BNP 310 pg/mL, down from 480 at last visit. Echocardiogram 2 months ago demonstrated EF 33%. On furosemide 40mg daily, carvedilol 12.5mg BID, lisinopril 10mg daily, and spironolactone 25mg daily. Impression: chronic systolic heart failure, compensated.
notes: I50.22 = chronic systolic heart failure; EF 33% confirms systolic dysfunction. Patient is compensated so I50.21 (acute) is inappropriate. Use I50.23 only when acute decompensation is superimposed on a chronic baseline.

note: 34-year-old nonsmoker with moderate persistent asthma presenting for scheduled follow-up. Uses albuterol inhaler 3–4 times per week for symptomatic relief. No nighttime awakenings in the past month. Spirometry today: FEV1 79% predicted, bronchodilator response 13%. Currently on fluticasone 110mcg/actuation BID. ACT score 17 (not well controlled). No acute exacerbation today. Plan: step up to fluticasone-salmeterol combination inhaler and recheck in 6 weeks.
notes: J45.40 = moderate persistent asthma, uncomplicated. SABA use 3–4 days/week and suboptimal ACT score place this in the moderate persistent category. No exacerbation is occurring at this visit, so J45.41 (with acute exacerbation) is not appropriate.

note: 58-year-old male presenting with 5-day history of productive cough with yellow sputum, fever to 102.1F, and right-sided pleuritic chest pain. No hospitalization or antibiotic use in the past 90 days. Chest X-ray: right lower lobe consolidation. WBC 14,200. O2 sat 95% on room air. Sputum culture pending. Diagnosed with community-acquired pneumonia; started on azithromycin 500mg daily for 5 days.
notes: J18.9 = pneumonia, unspecified organism — correct when causative pathogen is not yet confirmed. Code updates to an organism-specific code (e.g., J13 for S. pneumoniae, J15.1 for Pseudomonas) once culture results are available.

note: 24-year-old woman presents with 2-day history of dysuria, urinary frequency, and suprapubic discomfort. No fever, no flank pain, no nausea or vomiting. Urinalysis: positive leukocyte esterase, positive nitrites, >50 WBC/hpf. Urine culture sent. Diagnosed with uncomplicated lower urinary tract infection. Prescribed nitrofurantoin 100mg BID for 5 days.
notes: N39.0 = urinary tract infection, site not specified. No fever or costovertebral angle tenderness to suggest pyelonephritis (N10). The word 'uncomplicated' in the note is a clinical descriptor, not a separate codeable finding.

note: 52-year-old woman presenting with 3-month history of progressive fatigue and exertional dyspnea. CBC: hemoglobin 9.2 g/dL, MCV 71 fL (microcytic). Iron studies: ferritin 5 ng/mL, serum iron 38 mcg/dL, TIBC 432 mcg/dL. No overt GI blood loss reported. Impression: iron deficiency anemia. Started on ferrous sulfate 325mg TID. Referred to gastroenterology to evaluate for an occult source.
notes: D50.9 = iron deficiency anemia, unspecified — labs confirm classic pattern (low ferritin, low serum iron, elevated TIBC, microcytosis). Source not yet identified so an etiology-specific sub-code (e.g., D50.0 for blood loss) is not yet applicable.

note: 48-year-old woman presenting with 4-month history of fatigue, 12-pound weight gain, cold intolerance, and constipation. TSH 14.2 mIU/L (reference 0.4–4.0 mIU/L); free T4 0.6 ng/dL (low). No prior thyroid disease history. Thyroid gland non-enlarged on exam. Diagnosis: primary hypothyroidism. Initiated levothyroxine 50mcg daily; TSH to be rechecked in 6 weeks.
notes: E03.9 = hypothyroidism, unspecified — appropriate for newly diagnosed primary hypothyroidism without confirmed etiology. Anti-TPO not yet checked, so autoimmune/Hashimoto's (E06.3) and post-surgical hypothyroidism (E89.0) cannot be coded at this visit.

note: 29-year-old male presenting with a 7-month history of excessive, difficult-to-control worry about work, finances, and family. Reports associated muscle tension, irritability, difficulty concentrating, and disrupted sleep. No history of panic attacks. No substance use. GAD-7 score 14 (moderate-severe). Symptoms cause significant occupational impairment. Diagnosis: generalized anxiety disorder. Initiating sertraline 25mg with plan to titrate; referring to cognitive behavioral therapy.
notes: F41.1 = generalized anxiety disorder. Pervasive, difficult-to-control worry across multiple domains for >6 months with somatic symptoms and functional impairment satisfies DSM-5/ICD-10 criteria. No panic attacks present to code F41.0 (panic disorder).

note: 61-year-old male with type 2 diabetes mellitus presenting for follow-up. Reports 6-month history of bilateral foot numbness and burning pain, worse at night. Monofilament testing shows diminished plantar sensation bilaterally. Nerve conduction study confirms peripheral polyneuropathy. HbA1c 7.8%. Patient also screens positive for depression; PHQ-9 score 12. Known history of recurrent MDD, last episode 18 months ago. Denies suicidal ideation. Initiating duloxetine 30mg daily for both neuropathic pain and depression.
notes: E11.40 = T2DM with diabetic neuropathy, unspecified — ICD-10 combination code captures both diabetes and neuropathic complication; do not code the polyneuropathy separately with a G code. F33.1 = MDD, recurrent, moderate (PHQ-9 12). That duloxetine addresses both conditions does not affect coding.

note: 77-year-old woman with COPD (GOLD 2, stable), long-standing persistent atrial fibrillation, and chronic diastolic heart failure presenting for quarterly follow-up. No acute dyspnea, no palpitations, no worsening of lower extremity edema. Rate-controlled on diltiazem 120mg daily; INR 2.4 (therapeutic on warfarin). Echocardiogram 3 months ago: EF 58%, grade II diastolic dysfunction. Spirometry: FEV1/FVC 0.62, FEV1 58% predicted. On tiotropium 18mcg daily and fluticasone-vilanterol. Impression: all three chronic conditions stable.
notes: J44.9 = COPD, unspecified; stable, no exacerbation or infection. I48.11 = long-standing persistent AF (duration >12 months, not yet classified permanent). I50.32 = chronic diastolic heart failure; EF preserved but grade II diastolic dysfunction on echo establishes the diagnosis. All three are independently active and each is coded.

note: 44-year-old man referred for weight management evaluation. BMI 43.2 kg/m². Diagnosed with obstructive sleep apnea 2 years ago on prior sleep study (AHI 38), currently non-adherent to CPAP. Type 2 diabetes mellitus diagnosed 3 years ago; HbA1c 6.9% on metformin 1000mg BID, no documented diabetic complications. Lipid panel and renal function within normal limits. Referred to bariatric surgery program and re-educated on CPAP adherence.
notes: E66.01 = morbid (severe) obesity due to excess calories (BMI >= 40); more specific than E66.9. G47.33 = obstructive sleep apnea — code the condition itself; non-adherence to CPAP does not negate the diagnosis. E11.9 = T2DM without complications; A1c at goal and no diabetic complications documented.

note: 63-year-old woman with coronary artery disease (cardiac catheterization 4 years ago showing 70% LAD stenosis, managed medically without revascularization), hypertension, and hyperlipidemia presenting for annual cardiovascular review. No chest pain, no exertional dyspnea. BP 128/76 on amlodipine 10mg and ramipril 10mg. LDL 68 mg/dL on rosuvastatin 40mg. EKG: normal sinus rhythm, no ischemic changes. All conditions well controlled.
notes: I25.10 = atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina pectoris; catheterization-confirmed CAD coded here. No chest pain, so angina codes (I25.11x) are not appropriate. I10 = essential hypertension. E78.5 = hyperlipidemia, unspecified. All three are chronic conditions active at this visit.

note: 19-year-old college student with longstanding mild persistent asthma, allergic rhinitis, and atopic dermatitis presenting for combined allergy and pulmonology follow-up. Uses albuterol 1–2 times per week, no nighttime awakenings; on fluticasone 110mcg/actuation BID. ACT score 20 (well controlled), no exacerbation today. Seasonal nasal congestion and sneezing controlled on loratadine 10mg daily; specific allergen not confirmed by testing. Eczema patches on bilateral antecubital fossae, managed with triamcinolone 0.1% ointment.
notes: J45.30 = mild persistent asthma, uncomplicated; SABA 1–2x/week is mild persistent, no exacerbation at this visit. J30.9 = allergic rhinitis, unspecified; seasonal pattern noted but allergen not confirmed by testing, so a specific allergen code (e.g., J30.1) is not appropriate. L20.9 = atopic dermatitis, unspecified.

note: 69-year-old man presenting with 2-day history of acute swelling, erythema, and severe pain of the right first metatarsophalangeal joint. Gout attributed to impaired urate excretion from chronic kidney disease. Serum uric acid 9.2 mg/dL. eGFR 36 mL/min/1.73m² (CKD stage 3b). History of hypertension, well controlled on amlodipine 5mg. Colchicine 0.6mg BID initiated; allopurinol dose adjusted for renal function. Impression: acute gout flare of right foot due to renal impairment, hypertensive CKD stage 3b.
notes: M10.371 = gout due to renal impairment, right ankle and foot — ICD-10 provides an etiology-specific gout code when CKD is the established cause; do not use idiopathic gout code M10.071. I12.9 = hypertensive CKD stage 1–4; ICD-10 presumes a causal link when hypertension and CKD coexist — code I12.9, not separate I10 + N18.x. N18.32 = CKD stage 3b (eGFR 30–44 mL/min) required as an additional code alongside I12.9.

note: 82-year-old woman brought from a nursing facility with 1-day history of acute confusion, fever to 103.2F, and heart rate 118. Urinalysis: >100 WBC/hpf, positive nitrites. Empiric piperacillin-tazobactam started. Blood and urine cultures drawn; blood cultures finalized at 48 hours growing Escherichia coli. Lactate 1.4 mmol/L. SOFA score 2. Impression: sepsis due to E. coli with urinary tract source.
notes: A41.51 = sepsis due to Escherichia coli — organism-specific code applied once cultures confirm E. coli; preferred over A41.9 (unspecified). N39.0 = UTI, coded as the infectious source and sequenced after the sepsis code per ICD-10-CM sepsis sequencing guidelines. E. coli bacteremia is not coded separately when sepsis is the principal diagnosis.

note: 51-year-old male with 20-year history of heavy daily alcohol use presenting with increasing abdominal girth and fatigue. Continues to consume approximately 8–10 drinks daily; meets DSM-5 criteria for severe alcohol use disorder. Abdominal ultrasound: nodular liver consistent with cirrhosis, splenomegaly; no ascites. Platelets 68,000/uL (low), consistent with hypersplenism from portal hypertension. AST 92, ALT 47, total bilirubin 2.1 mg/dL. Referred to hepatology; alcohol cessation counseled.
notes: F10.20 = alcohol use disorder, moderate/severe, uncomplicated (no concurrent withdrawal or intoxication episode coded here). K70.30 = alcoholic cirrhosis of liver without ascites; ascites absent on imaging distinguishes K70.30 from K70.31. D69.59 = other secondary thrombocytopenia; hypersplenism from portal hypertension is the mechanism, making this more precise than D69.6 (unspecified thrombocytopenia).
